meaning: n.灏藉姏,鍔姏,鍙戞尌,琛屼娇,杩愮敤
examples: Climbing further, with renewed courage, he at last saw his goal before him, but not without long and weary exertion.
He clearly found the physical exertion exhilarating.
She was hot and breathless from the exertion of cycling uphill.
Lewis admitted that harvesting a deer can be incredibly hard because of the physical exertion.
The club said it contained information about players' fitness and measures of exertion levels.
